Heroes A · Midnight editorial B · Light split + panel C · Proof-first metrics

Approach A

Credibility that compounds in public.

Open with authority: midnight band, tight display measure, lede in softened inverse ink. Best when the promise is existential or stakes-led — not when you need product UI in the first fold.

Start with a walkthrough Read the evidence

Approach B

Show the offer and the guardrails together.

Light base keeps the page airy; the right column is an elevated card (ghost lift) for concrete deliverables, syllabus bullets, or “what you get” lists. Dense meta rows may use border-top/border-bottom per DESIGN.md exceptions.

See pricing How it works

Included in every build

A checklist reads faster than a paragraph. Keep each line one idea; icons optional.

  • Editorial IA — routes, section cadence, and conversion spine.
  • Voice layer — corpus-linked tone, disclosure, and review loops.
  • Systems wiring — auth, content, and analytics without lock-in.
94% Leaders surveyed say “plausible polish” eroded trust in their field (sample illustrative).
12 wks Typical cadence from discovery to first public ship for a scoped platform slice.
0 Hidden model training on your corpus without a signed retention policy.

Approach C

Let the numbers earn the headline.

Proof-first heroes work when your audience arrives skeptical. Keep claims verifiable; pair with an evidence band or methodology link. Surface ramp: section tiles → card metrics → display headline on the same tonal family.

How we count Primary sources

Related full-page exemplars

For full-bleed cinematic height, product meta strip, or scroll-linked narrative, use the dedicated files exemplar-hero-cinematic, exemplar-hero-product-split, and exemplar-hero-scroll-narrative — each isolates one pattern for Stitch → React migration.

Patterns

  • Feature sections
  • Data visualization
